# Advanced Time Tracking Specification

**Document Version:** 2.0
**Last Updated:** November 2025
**Target Implementation:** Q2-Q3 2026
**Status:** Specification Complete - Ready for Implementation
**Priority:** High (Essential for professional use cases)

---

## Executive Summary

This document outlines advanced time tracking features to complement the existing basic timer functionality in Neuman Brain. While basic start/stop timer functionality exists, this spec covers professional-grade features modeled after Toggl Track, Clockify, and Harvest to make Neuman Brain competitive for freelancers, agencies, and teams who need comprehensive time tracking.

### What Exists (Current State)
- ✅ Basic time tracking widget
- ✅ Start/stop timer functionality
- ✅ Time entries list

### What This Spec Adds (Advanced Features)
- ⏳ **Professional reporting** - Weekly/monthly reports, billable hours
- ⏳ **Advanced entry management** - Manual entries, bulk editing, templates
- ⏳ **Project management** - Projects, clients, budgets, rates
- ⏳ **Productivity features** - Idle detection, Pomodoro timer
- ⏳ **Export & integration** - CSV/PDF export, import from Toggl/Clockify
- ⏳ **Calendar integration** - Visual timeline of tracked time

---

## 1. Competitive Analysis

### 1.1 Toggl Track (Industry Leader)

**Pricing:** Free (5 projects), Starter ($10/mo), Premium ($20/mo)
**Market Position:** Most popular time tracking tool for freelancers and agencies

#### Core Features
| Feature | Description | Priority |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| **One-Click Timer** | Start/stop tracking instantly from anywhere | P0 (✅ Have) |
| **Manual Time Entry** | Add time retroactively with specific start/end times | P0 (⏳ Need) |
| **Project Organization** | Hierarchical: Workspace → Client → Project → Task | P0 (⏳ Need) |
| **Idle Detection** | Prompts to keep/discard idle time after inactivity | P1 (⏳ Need) |
| **Calendar View** | Visual timeline of all tracked time | P1 (⏳ Need) |
| **Reporting** | Custom reports with filters, exports (PDF, CSV, Excel) | P0 (⏳ Need) |
| **Billable Rates** | Assign hourly rates to projects/tasks | P1 (⏳ Need) |
| **Tags** | Categorize time entries with custom tags | P1 (⏳ Need) |
| **Offline Mode** | Track time without internet, sync later | P0 (✅ Have - IndexedDB) |
| **Pomodoro Timer** | Built-in focus timer with breaks | P2 (⏳ Need) |

### 1.2 Feature Comparison Matrix

| Feature | Toggl | Clockify | Harvest | RescueTime | Neuman Brain Target |
|---------|-------|----------|---------|------------|---------------------|
| **Price (Free)** | 5 projects | ∞ projects | 1 project | Basic reports | ∞ projects |
| **Real-time Timer** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ (have) |
| **Manual Entry** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ⏳ (need) |
| **Projects** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ⏳ (need) |
| **Tasks** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ (Kanban integration) |
| **Tags** | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ⏳ (need) |
| **Idle Detection** | ✅ Desktop | ✅ Desktop | ❌ | ✅ | ⏳ (P2) |
| **Reports** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ⏳ (need) |
| **Billable Rates** | ✅ | ✅ Paid | ✅ | ❌ | ⏳ (P2) |
| **Invoicing** | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ⏳ (P3) |
| **Calendar View** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ⏳ (need) |
| **Offline Mode** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ (via IndexedDB) |
| **Export** | CSV/PDF/Excel | CSV/PDF | CSV/XLS | CSV | ⏳ (need CSV/JSON) |

**Neuman Brain Advantages:**
- ✅ 100% free (no project limits)
- ✅ Privacy-first (local-first, no cloud required)
- ✅ Integrated with tasks/projects (no app switching)
- ✅ Open source

---

## 2. Data Architecture

### 2.1 Enhanced Data Model

Based on Toggl's proven structure with Neuman Brain enhancements:

```typescript
// Core Types (extend existing if basic types exist)

interface TimeEntry {
  id: string;                    // UUID
  workspaceId: string;          // Always use default workspace for now
  projectId?: string;           // Optional: Link to project
  taskId?: string;              // Optional: Link to Kanban task
  description: string;          // Title/what you worked on (required)
  notes?: string;               // Additional details

  tags: string[];               // Categorization (e.g., "meeting", "coding", "research")

  // Time tracking
  startTime: string;            // ISO 8601 timestamp
  endTime?: string;             // ISO 8601 timestamp (null = running timer)
  duration: number;             // Seconds (calculated or manual)

  // Billing (Phase 2)
  billable: boolean;            // Is this billable time?
  hourlyRate?: number;          // USD per hour

  // Metadata
  createdAt: string;            // ISO 8601
  updatedAt: string;            // ISO 8601
  userId?: string;              // For multi-user support (future)

  // UI state (non-persisted)
  isRunning?: boolean;          // Computed: endTime === null
}

interface Project {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  color: string;                // Hex color for visual distinction
  clientName?: string;          // Optional: Company/client name

  // Budget tracking
  estimatedHours?: number;      // Total estimated hours
  hourlyRate?: number;          // Default rate for this project

  // Status
  active: boolean;              // Show in active list
  archived: boolean;            // Hide from UI

  // Metadata
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;

  // Computed (non-persisted)
  totalTrackedTime?: number;    // Sum of all time entries
  completionPercentage?: number; // trackedTime / estimatedHours * 100
}

interface TimeTrackingState {
  // Current timer
  activeEntry: TimeEntry | null;     // Currently running timer

  // History
  entries: TimeEntry[];               // All time entries (sorted desc by startTime)
  projects: Project[];                // All projects

  // UI state
  selectedDate: string;               // Date filter for calendar view
  selectedProject: string | null;     // Project filter
  selectedTags: string[];             // Tag filters
  viewMode: 'list' | 'calendar' | 'report'; // Current view

  // Settings
  defaultProjectId?: string;          // Quick-start default
  reminderEnabled: boolean;           // Remind if not tracking (future)
  idleDetectionMinutes: number;       // Idle timeout (0 = disabled)
  roundingMinutes: 0 | 5 | 15 | 30;  // Round time entries
}
```

### 2.2 Storage Strategy

**Primary Storage:** IndexedDB (already in use)
- **Why:** Time tracking generates lots of data (1000+ entries per year)
- **Benefit:** Efficient queries, indexes, offline support
- **Size Limit:** ~50GB+ (sufficient for decades of time entries)

**Backup Storage:** localStorage (active timer only)
- Ensures current timer persists even if IndexedDB fails
- Fallback for older browsers

**IndexedDB Schema:**
```typescript
// Extend existing indexedDB service or create new tables

db.version(X).stores({
  timeEntries: 'id, projectId, taskId, startTime, endTime, [projectId+startTime]',
  timeProjects: 'id, name, active'
});
```

---

## 3. Feature Specifications

### 3.1 Phase 1: Manual Entry & Projects (15h)

**Priority:** P0 - Core features
**Dependencies:** Basic timer already exists

#### 3.1.1 Manual Time Entry Modal (8h)

**Location:** `src/widgets/TimeTracking/ManualEntry.tsx`

**Features:**
- Date picker (defaults to today)
- Start time input (HH:MM format with AM/PM)
- End time input (HH:MM format with AM/PM)
- Duration calculator (auto-calculates from start/end, or enter directly)
- Description text input (required)
- Notes textarea (optional)
- Project selector
- Tag multi-select
- Billable toggle

**Validation:**
- End time must be after start time
- Duration must be > 0 and < 24 hours
- Can't overlap with existing entries (warning, not error)
- Description required (min 3 characters)

**UX Flow:**
1. Click "Add Time Manually" button
2. Modal opens with smart defaults (today, current time)
3. Fill in details
4. Save → Entry added to list, modal closes

#### 3.1.2 Project Management (7h)

**Location:** `src/widgets/TimeTracking/Projects.tsx`

**Features:**
- Create project modal (name, color, client, hourly rate)
- List all projects with:
  - Total time tracked
  - Estimated hours (if set)
  - Progress bar
  - Archive/Delete actions
- Edit project details
- Color picker (8 preset colors + custom)
- Archive old projects (hide from dropdowns but keep data)

**Project Colors (8 presets):**
- Magenta: `#E91E8C` (brand primary)
- Cyan: `#00C9FF` (brand secondary)
- Purple: `#9333EA`
- Green: `#10B981`
- Orange: `#F59E0B`
- Red: `#EF4444`
- Blue: `#3B82F6`
- Gray: `#6B7280`

---

### 3.2 Phase 2: Reporting & Analytics (15h)

**Priority:** P0 - Required for weekly hour booking
**Dependencies:** Phase 1 complete

#### 3.2.1 Weekly Summary Report (8h)

**Location:** `src/widgets/TimeTracking/Reports/WeeklySummary.tsx`

**Features:**
- Calendar week selector (defaults to current week)
- **Summary stats:**
  - Total hours worked
  - Billable vs. non-billable split
  - Hours by project (pie chart)
  - Hours by day (bar chart)
- **Detailed breakdown:**
  - Table: Project | Mon | Tue | Wed | Thu | Fri | Sat | Sun | Total
  - Click any cell → see entries for that day/project
- **Export options:**
  - Copy to clipboard (formatted text)
  - Download CSV
  - Download PDF (future)
  - Print view

**Use Case:** End of week hour booking
- Select week → See all time by project → Copy/export for timesheet entry

#### 3.2.2 Monthly Report (4h)

**Features:**
- Month/year selector
- Total hours worked
- Average hours per day
- Most tracked project
- Most productive day
- Trend chart (daily totals)
- Export to CSV

#### 3.2.3 Project Report (3h)

**Features:**
- Single project deep-dive
- Total time tracked
- Time per task (if linked to Kanban)
- Timeline visualization
- Budget tracking (if estimated hours set)
- Export project data

---

### 3.3 Phase 3: Calendar View (10h)

**Priority:** P1 - High value visualization
**Dependencies:** Phase 1 complete

#### 3.3.1 Day/Week Calendar View (10h)

**Location:** `src/widgets/TimeTracking/CalendarView.tsx`

**Features:**
- Day view: 24-hour timeline with time blocks
- Week view: 7-column grid with time blocks
- Drag time entries to adjust start time
- Resize time entries to adjust duration
- Click empty space → create new entry
- Color-coded by project
- Tooltips on hover (show description, project)
- Sync with existing Calendar widget

**Implementation:**
- Reuse existing Calendar widget infrastructure
- Add time-block rendering layer
- Integrate with TimeEntry store

---

### 3.4 Phase 4: Advanced Features (20h)

**Priority:** P2 - Nice to have
**Dependencies:** Phases 1-3 complete

#### 3.4.1 Idle Detection (5h)

**Features:**
- Detect when computer is idle (via Page Visibility API)
- After X minutes (configurable), show modal:
  - "You've been idle for 15 minutes"
  - Option 1: Keep idle time
  - Option 2: Discard idle time
  - Option 3: Stop timer
- Works in PWA and desktop

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// Page Visibility API for idle detection
let idleStartTime: number | null = null;

document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', () => {
  if (document.hidden) {
    // User switched away - start idle timer
    idleStartTime = Date.now();
  } else {
    // User came back - check idle duration
    if (idleStartTime) {
      const idleMinutes = (Date.now() - idleStartTime) / 1000 / 60;
      if (idleMinutes > settings.idleDetectionMinutes) {
        showIdleModal(idleMinutes);
      }
      idleStartTime = null;
    }
  }
});
```

#### 3.4.2 Pomodoro Timer (5h)

**Features:**
- 25-minute focus sessions
- 5-minute breaks
- Automatic time entry creation after each session
- Desktop notifications when session ends
- Configurable durations

**UX:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Pomodoro Timer                     │
│  ────────────────────────────────   │
│  🍅 Focus Time: 25:00               │
│  ────────────────────────────────   │
│  [Start Focus Session]              │
│                                     │
│  Sessions today: ●●●○○ (3/5)        │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### 3.4.3 Keyboard Shortcuts (3h)

**Shortcuts:**
- `T` - Start/stop timer
- `M` - Add manual entry
- `P` - Open projects
- `R` - Open reports
- `Cmd/Ctrl + K` - Quick search entries
- `Cmd/Ctrl + N` - New project
- `Esc` - Close modals

#### 3.4.4 Billable Rates & Revenue (7h)

**Features:**
- Set hourly rates per project
- Mark entries as billable/non-billable
- Calculate total revenue in reports
- Revenue trends over time
- Client-specific billing reports

**Revenue Calculation:**
```typescript
function calculateRevenue(entries: TimeEntry[], projects: Project[]): number {
  return entries.reduce((total, entry) => {
    if (!entry.billable) return total;

    const rate = entry.hourlyRate ||
                 projects.find(p => p.id === entry.projectId)?.hourlyRate ||
                 0;

    const hours = entry.duration / 3600;
    return total + (hours * rate);
  }, 0);
}
```

---

## 4. Time Utilities & Helpers

### 4.1 Core Time Calculations

```typescript
// src/widgets/TimeTracking/utils/timeCalculations.ts

/**
 * Format duration in seconds to human-readable string
 * @example formatDuration(3665) => "1h 1m 5s"
 * @example formatDuration(3665, 'short') => "1:01:05"
 */
export function formatDuration(
  seconds: number,
  format: 'long' | 'short' = 'long'
): string {
  const hours = Math.floor(seconds / 3600);
  const minutes = Math.floor((seconds % 3600) / 60);
  const secs = seconds % 60;

  if (format === 'short') {
    return `${hours.toString().padStart(2, '0')}:${minutes.toString().padStart(2, '0')}:${secs.toString().padStart(2, '0')}`;
  }

  const parts = [];
  if (hours > 0) parts.push(`${hours}h`);
  if (minutes > 0) parts.push(`${minutes}m`);
  if (secs > 0 || parts.length === 0) parts.push(`${secs}s`);

  return parts.join(' ');
}

/**
 * Calculate duration between two timestamps
 */
export function calculateDuration(startTime: string, endTime: string): number {
  const start = new Date(startTime).getTime();
  const end = new Date(endTime).getTime();
  return Math.floor((end - start) / 1000);
}

/**
 * Round duration to nearest interval
 * @example roundDuration(3665, 15) => 3600 (round to 1h)
 */
export function roundDuration(seconds: number, roundToMinutes: number): number {
  if (roundToMinutes === 0) return seconds;

  const roundToSeconds = roundToMinutes * 60;
  return Math.round(seconds / roundToSeconds) * roundToSeconds;
}

/**
 * Get week date range
 */
export function getWeekRange(date: Date): { start: Date; end: Date } {
  const start = new Date(date);
  const day = start.getDay();
  const diff = start.getDate() - day + (day === 0 ? -6 : 1); // Monday as first day
  start.setDate(diff);
  start.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0);

  const end = new Date(start);
  end.setDate(end.getDate() + 6);
  end.setHours(23, 59, 59, 999);

  return { start, end };
}

/**
 * Group entries by day
 */
export function groupEntriesByDay(entries: TimeEntry[]): Record<string, TimeEntry[]> {
  return entries.reduce((groups, entry) => {
    const date = new Date(entry.startTime).toISOString().split('T')[0];
    if (!groups[date]) groups[date] = [];
    groups[date].push(entry);
    return groups;
  }, {} as Record<string, TimeEntry[]>);
}

/**
 * Export entries to CSV
 */
export function exportToCSV(entries: TimeEntry[], projects: Project[]): string {
  const projectMap = Object.fromEntries(projects.map(p => [p.id, p.name]));

  const headers = ['Date', 'Start Time', 'End Time', 'Duration', 'Description', 'Project', 'Tags', 'Billable'];
  const rows = entries.map(entry => {
    const start = new Date(entry.startTime);
    const end = entry.endTime ? new Date(entry.endTime) : null;

    return [
      start.toLocaleDateString(),
      start.toLocaleTimeString(),
      end ? end.toLocaleTimeString() : 'Running',
      formatDuration(entry.duration, 'short'),
      `"${entry.description}"`,
      entry.projectId ? projectMap[entry.projectId] || 'Unknown' : 'No Project',
      entry.tags.join(', '),
      entry.billable ? 'Yes' : 'No'
    ].join(',');
  });

  return [headers.join(','), ...rows].join('\n');
}
```

---

## 5. Integration with Existing Features

### 5.1 Kanban Board Integration

**Location:** `src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts`

Add time tracking to existing tasks:

```typescript
interface Task {
  // ... existing fields

  // New fields
  timeTracked?: number;        // Total seconds tracked (computed)
  timeEstimate?: number;       // Estimated duration in seconds
  activeTimerEntryId?: string; // Link to active timer
}

// New actions in useKanbanStore
interface KanbanStore {
  // ... existing actions

  startTaskTimer: (taskId: string) => void;
  stopTaskTimer: (taskId: string) => void;
  getTaskTimeTracked: (taskId: string) => number;
}
```

**UI Changes:**
- Add "Start Timer" button to task cards
- Show total time tracked on task cards
- Show time estimate vs. actual indicator
- Link to time entries from task detail view

### 5.2 Calendar Widget Integration

**Location:** `src/widgets/Calendar/CalendarWidget.tsx`

Display time entries on calendar:

```typescript
// Add time entry layer to calendar
interface CalendarEvent {
  // ... existing fields
  type: 'task' | 'timeEntry';
  timeEntry?: TimeEntry; // If type === 'timeEntry'
}
```

**UI Changes:**
- Show time entries as blocks on calendar
- Different visual style than tasks (e.g., striped pattern)
- Click time entry → open time tracking detail
- Option to toggle time entry visibility

---

## 6. Import/Export & Data Portability

### 6.1 Import from Toggl

**Implementation:** CSV import tool

**Steps:**
1. Export data from Toggl (Settings → Data Export → Download CSV)
2. In Neuman Brain: Time Tracking → Import → Select Toggl CSV
3. Map fields (automatic for standard Toggl format)
4. Preview import (show sample entries)
5. Confirm → Import all entries
6. Verify imported data

**Data Mapping:**
```
Toggl CSV → Neuman Brain
────────────────────────
Project → Project (create if not exists)
Description → description
Start date + Start time → startTime
End date + End time → endTime
Duration → duration (calculated)
Tags → tags (split by comma)
Billable → billable
```

### 6.2 Export Formats

**CSV Export:**
- All time entries with full details
- Project-specific exports
- Date range exports

**JSON Export:**
- Complete backup (all entries + projects)
- Part of .brain file format

**PDF Export (Phase 4):**
- Weekly/monthly reports
- Invoice-ready format
- Client-facing reports

---

## 7. Performance Considerations

### 7.1 Optimization Strategies

**Database:**
- Index on `startTime` for fast sorting
- Compound index on `projectId + startTime` for filtered queries
- Virtual scrolling for entry list (render only visible items)
- Paginated loading (50 entries at a time)

**State Management:**
- Don't store all entries in memory (use DB queries)
- Debounce search input (300ms)
- Memoize expensive calculations (React.useMemo)
- Lazy load reports only when viewed

**Bundle Size:**
- Code split time tracking widget (~15KB gzipped)
- Lazy load reporting components (~8KB gzipped)
- Use native Date API (no moment.js)
- Minimize chart library size (use lightweight recharts or Chart.js)

**Target Metrics:**
- Initial load: < 100ms
- Start/stop timer: < 50ms
- Load 1000 entries: < 500ms
- Generate report: < 1s
- Export CSV: < 500ms

---

## 8. Testing Strategy

### 8.1 Unit Tests (Vitest)

**Test Coverage Targets:**
- Time calculations: 100%
- Store actions: 90%
- Database queries: 85%

**Example Tests:**
```typescript
describe('formatDuration', () => {
  it('formats seconds correctly', () => {
    expect(formatDuration(3665)).toBe('1h 1m 5s');
    expect(formatDuration(3665, 'short')).toBe('01:01:05');
  });

  it('handles edge cases', () => {
    expect(formatDuration(0)).toBe('0s');
    expect(formatDuration(59)).toBe('59s');
    expect(formatDuration(3600)).toBe('1h');
  });
});
```

### 8.2 Integration Tests

**Test Scenarios:**
1. Start timer → stop timer → entry saved to DB
2. Create manual entry → appears in list
3. Filter entries by project → correct entries shown
4. Generate weekly report → accurate calculations
5. Export to CSV → valid format

---

## 9. Implementation Roadmap

### Phase 1: Manual Entry & Projects (15h) - Q2 2026
- Manual time entry modal
- Project management
- Tag system

### Phase 2: Reporting & Analytics (15h) - Q2 2026
- Weekly summary report
- Monthly report
- Project report
- CSV export

### Phase 3: Calendar View (10h) - Q3 2026
- Day/week calendar view
- Drag-and-drop editing
- Integration with existing Calendar widget

### Phase 4: Advanced Features (20h) - Q3 2026
- Idle detection
- Pomodoro timer
- Keyboard shortcuts
- Billable rates & revenue

**Total Effort:** 60 hours across Q2-Q3 2026

---

## 10. Success Metrics

### Adoption Metrics

**Phase 1-2 (Core Features):**
- 30% of active users use time tracking weekly
- Average 5+ time entries per active user per week
- 60% of time tracking users generate reports weekly

### Usage Metrics

**Engagement:**
- Average session duration: 2+ hours
- Time entries per day: 3-8
- Projects per user: 2-5
- Weekly report views: 1-2

**Performance:**
- Time to start timer: < 2 clicks, < 5 seconds
- Report generation time: < 1 second
- Zero data loss incidents

---

## 11. Competitive Positioning

### Unique Value Propositions

**vs. Toggl:**
- ✅ Free (Toggl: $10/mo for decent features)
- ✅ Integrated with task management (no switching apps)
- ✅ Privacy-first (local storage, no required cloud)
- ✅ Open source

**vs. Clockify:**
- ✅ Better UX (Clockify can be clunky)
- ✅ Integrated with PM tools
- ✅ Faster performance (no server roundtrips)

**vs. Harvest:**
- ✅ Free (Harvest: $12/mo)
- ✅ Integrated PM + time tracking
- ❌ No invoicing (future feature)

**Marketing Positioning:**

**Tagline:** "Track your time where you already work"

**Key Messages:**
1. Stop switching between apps - time tracking built into your productivity platform
2. Privacy-first - your time data stays on your device
3. Free forever - no artificial limits
4. Beautiful, fast, modern - not another clunky time tracker

---

## 12. Related Documentation

- [Implementation Plan](implementation_plan.md) - Q1-Q4 2026 sprint breakdown
- [Feature Roadmap](../product/feature_roadmap.md) - Overall product plan
- [Import/Export Spec](import_export_spec.md) - Data portability (includes time entries)
- [Product Vision](../product/product_vision.md) - Strategic direction

---

## 13. Appendix: Research Sources

- Toggl Track Documentation: https://toggl.com/track/features/
- Toggl Track API: https://developers.track.toggl.com/
- Clockify Features: https://clockify.me/features
- Harvest Time Tracking: https://www.getharvest.com/
- RescueTime: https://www.rescuetime.com/
- IndexedDB Best Practices: https://web.dev/indexeddb/

---

**Document Status:** ✅ Specification Complete - Ready for Q2-Q3 2026 Implementation
**Last Updated:** November 2025
**Next Review:** When starting advanced time tracking implementation (Q2 2026)

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Notes Type Definitions
     3→ *
     4→ * Core types for the Notes MVP feature
     5→ * Designed to be extensible for future enhancements
     6→ */
     7→
     8→/**
     9→ * Note - Individual note document
    10→ *
    11→ * Storage: IndexedDB via Zustand persist
    12→ * Format: Lexical JSON state in content field
    13→ */
    14→export interface Note {
    15→  /** Unique identifier (UUID v4) */
    16→  id: string;
    17→
    18→  /** Parent folder ID (null = root level) */
    19→  folderId: string | null;
    20→
    21→  /** Note title (plain text) */
    22→  title: string;
    23→
    24→  /** Rich text content (Lexical JSON state) */
    25→  content: string;
    26→
    27→  /** Plain text version of content (for search indexing) */
    28→  contentText: string;
    29→
    30→  /** Optional tags for categorization */
    31→  tags: string[];
    32→
    33→  /** Creation timestamp */
    34→  createdAt: Date;
    35→
    36→  /** Last update timestamp */
    37→  updatedAt: Date;
    38→
    39→  /** Pin to top of folder */
    40→  isPinned: boolean;
    41→
    42→  /** Archive note (hide from main view) */
    43→  isArchived: boolean;
    44→
    45→  /** Optional color for note card */
    46→  color?: string;
    47→
    48→  /** Optional emoji icon */
    49→  icon?: string;
    50→
    51→  /**
    52→   * Future extensibility fields (not yet implemented)
    53→   * Uncomment when ready to use
    54→   */
    55→  // isFavorite?: boolean;           // Star/favorite notes
    56→  // isTemplate?: boolean;           // Mark as template
    57→  // linkedNotes?: string[];         // Backlinks to other notes
    58→  // mentions?: string[];            // @mentions of other notes
    59→  // attachments?: Attachment[];     // File attachments (future)
    60→  // collaborators?: string[];       // Shared with (future collaboration)
    61→  // version?: number;               // Version number for conflict resolution
    62→  // encryptionKey?: string;         // Encrypted note marker (Q2 2026)
    63→}
    64→
    65→/**
    66→ * Folder - Hierarchical folder structure
    67→ *
    68→ * Supports unlimited nesting depth
    69→ * Storage: IndexedDB via Zustand persist
    70→ */
    71→export interface Folder {
    72→  /** Unique identifier (UUID v4) */
    73→  id: string;
    74→
    75→  /** Parent folder ID (null = root level) */
    76→  parentId: string | null;
    77→
    78→  /** Folder name */
    79→  name: string;
    80→
    81→  /** Optional color (hex code) */
    82→  color?: string;
    83→
    84→  /** Optional emoji icon */
    85→  icon?: string;
    86→
    87→  /** Creation timestamp */
    88→  createdAt: Date;
    89→
    90→  /** Last update timestamp */
    91→  updatedAt: Date;
    92→
    93→  /** UI state - is folder expanded in tree view */
    94→  isExpanded: boolean;
    95→
    96→  /** Sort order within parent folder */
    97→  sortOrder?: number;
    98→
    99→  /**
   100→   * Future extensibility fields
   101→   */
   102→  // isShared?: boolean;             // Shared folder (future collaboration)
   103→  // permissions?: FolderPermissions; // Access control (future)
   104→  // template?: string;              // Default template for notes
   105→  // defaultTags?: string[];         // Auto-tag notes in this folder
   106→}
   107→
   108→/**
   109→ * Search Index Entry
   110→ *
   111→ * Stores searchable text for fast full-text search
   112→ * Storage: IndexedDB (separate from notes store)
   113→ */
   114→export interface SearchIndex {
   115→  /** Note ID this entry indexes */
   116→  noteId: string;
   117→
   118→  /** Searchable plain text (title + content) */
   119→  text: string;
   120→
   121→  /** When this entry was last indexed */
   122→  lastIndexed: Date;
   123→
   124→  /** Note title (for display in results) */
   125→  title: string;
   126→
   127→  /** Folder ID (for filtering search results) */
   128→  folderId: string | null;
   129→
   130→  /** First 200 chars of content (for snippets) */
   131→  snippet: string;
   132→}
   133→
   134→/**
   135→ * Note Sort Options
   136→ */
   137→export type NoteSortField = 'updatedAt' | 'createdAt' | 'title' | 'manual';
   138→export type NoteSortOrder = 'asc' | 'desc';
   139→
   140→export interface NoteSortConfig {
   141→  field: NoteSortField;
   142→  order: NoteSortOrder;
   143→}
   144→
   145→/**
   146→ * Note Filter Options
   147→ */
   148→export type NoteFilter = 'all' | 'pinned' | 'archived' | 'unarchived';
   149→
   150→/**
   151→ * Folder Tree Node (for rendering)
   152→ *
   153→ * Computed from Folder data
   154→ * Used for tree view rendering
   155→ */
   156→export interface FolderTreeNode extends Folder {
   157→  /** Nested children folders */
   158→  children: FolderTreeNode[];
   159→
   160→  /** Depth level (0 = root) */
   161→  depth: number;
   162→
   163→  /** Full path from root (for breadcrumbs) */
   164→  path: string[];
   165→
   166→  /** Number of notes in this folder (direct children only) */
   167→  noteCount: number;
   168→
   169→  /** Total notes including subfolders */
   170→  totalNoteCount: number;
   171→}
   172→
   173→/**
   174→ * Search Result
   175→ */
   176→export interface SearchResult {
   177→  /** Matching note */
   178→  note: Note;
   179→
   180→  /** Match score (0-1, higher = better match) */
   181→  score: number;
   182→
   183→  /** Highlighted snippet with search terms */
   184→  highlightedSnippet: string;
   185→
   186→  /** Matching indices for highlighting */
   187→  matches: Array<{
   188→    start: number;
   189→    end: number;
   190→  }>;
   191→}
   192→
   193→/**
   194→ * Editor State (UI state for editor component)
   195→ */
   196→export interface EditorState {
   197→  /** Currently open note ID (null = no note selected) */
   198→  activeNoteId: string | null;
   199→
   200→  /** Currently selected folder ID (null = root) */
   201→  activeFolderId: string | null;
   202→
   203→  /** Is editor in full-screen mode */
   204→  isFullscreen: boolean;
   205→
   206→  /** Is sidebar collapsed (mobile) */
   207→  isSidebarCollapsed: boolean;
   208→
   209→  /** Show note metadata panel */
   210→  showMetadata: boolean;
   211→
   212→  /** Character count */
   213→  characterCount: number;
   214→
   215→  /** Word count */
   216→  wordCount: number;
   217→
   218→  /** Is note being saved */
   219→  isSaving: boolean;
   220→
   221→  /** Last save timestamp */
   222→  lastSaved: Date | null;
   223→}
   224→
   225→/**
   226→ * Future: Attachment types (not yet implemented)
   227→ */
   228→export interface Attachment {
   229→  id: string;
   230→  noteId: string;
   231→  filename: string;
   232→  mimeType: string;
   233→  size: number; // bytes
   234→  url: string; // base64 data URL or file system handle
   235→  uploadedAt: Date;
   236→}
   237→
   238→/**
   239→ * Future: Note template (not yet implemented)
   240→ */
   241→export interface NoteTemplate {
   242→  id: string;
   243→  name: string;
   244→  content: string; // Lexical JSON state
   245→  icon?: string;
   246→  category?: string;
   247→  createdAt: Date;
   248→}
   249→
   250→/**
   251→ * Future: Backlink (bidirectional note linking)
   252→ */
   253→export interface Backlink {
   254→  sourceNoteId: string;
   255→  targetNoteId: string;
   256→  context: string; // Surrounding text where link appears
   257→  createdAt: Date;
   258→}
   259→
   260→/**
   261→ * Helper type: Partial note for updates
   262→ */
   263→export type NoteUpdate = Partial<Omit<Note, 'id' | 'createdAt'>>;
   264→
   265→/**
   266→ * Helper type: Partial folder for updates
   267→ */
   268→export type FolderUpdate = Partial<Omit<Folder, 'id' | 'createdAt'>>;
   269→
   270→/**
   271→ * Constants
   272→ */
   273→export const NOTE_CONSTANTS = {
   274→  /** Default new note title */
   275→  DEFAULT_TITLE: 'Untitled Note',
   276→
   277→  /** Max title length */
   278→  MAX_TITLE_LENGTH: 200,
   279→
   280→  /** Max content length (characters) */
   281→  MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH: 1000000, // 1 million chars
   282→
   283→  /** Max tags per note */
   284→  MAX_TAGS: 50,
   285→
   286→  /** Max tag length */
   287→  MAX_TAG_LENGTH: 50,
   288→
   289→  /** Search debounce (ms) */
   290→  SEARCH_DEBOUNCE_MS: 150,
   291→
   292→  /** Auto-save debounce (ms) */
   293→  AUTOSAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS: 2000,
   294→
   295→  /** Max search results */
   296→  MAX_SEARCH_RESULTS: 50,
   297→
   298→  /** Snippet length for search results */
   299→  SEARCH_SNIPPET_LENGTH: 200,
   300→} as const;
   301→
   302→export const FOLDER_CONSTANTS = {
   303→  /** Default new folder name */

# **COMPREHENSIVE FEATURE GAP REPORT**
## **Neuman Brain - Discussed vs. Implemented Features**

**Date:** November 18, 2025 (per system clock)  
**Analysis Scope:** All documentation in `docs/` + task files + source code inspection  
**Method:** Cross-referenced planned features in docs with actual implementation in `src/`

---

## **EXECUTIVE SUMMARY**

**Key Findings:**
- **125+ features shipped** (50% of 248 total planned features)

## **16. CONCLUSION**

### **Key Insights**

1. **Implementation ahead of docs:** Kanban has 98% Jira parity but docs say "Phase 5 planned"
2. **Specification quality is excellent:** 4 major specs (Automation, Import/Export, Gantt, Time Tracking) are production-ready
3. **Strategic gaps are clear:** Images (notes), manual entry (time tracking), wiki-links (notes), E2EE (privacy)
4. **Low-hanging fruit exists:** 9 features < 4h each, high user value

### **Feature Velocity Analysis**

- **Actual:** 125+ features shipped in ~140 hours (Sprint 1-7)
- **Velocity:** ~1.1 hours per feature average
- **Remaining:** 65 planned features ≈ 70-80 hours (at current velocity)
- **Projection:** Can complete all planned features by Q2 2026 end

### **Competitive Position**

**After Implementing Top 10 Priorities:**
- **Notes:** 55% → **75%** Obsidian (images, wiki-links)
- **Calendar:** 65% → **80%** Google Calendar (sync)
- **Kanban:** 65% → **90%** Linear (Gantt chart)
- **Time Tracking:** 23% → **70%** Toggl (manual entry, reports, projects)
- **Overall:** 50% → **80%** feature complete

**Market Position:** Privacy-first Notion alternative with **80% parity at 0% cost**.
